get.summary:
Health check. Performs a lightweight database ping. Returns 200 when healthy,
503 when the database is unreachable.

List all subjects who hold the given relation on an object.
Resolves subject sets recursively. Admin-only — use GET /v1/authz/subjects
for the permission-scoped view available to authenticated users.
Get the current runtime configuration.

post.summary:
Create an impersonation session for any user. Returns a session token that behaves like
a normal session but carries an `impersonated` flag in issued JWTs. Use this for admin
support workflows — not for production automation.

get.summary:
Look up a user by primary email address. Returns 400 if the `email` query parameter
is omitted.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Look up by primary email address (case-insensitive).

get.summary: Look up a user by ID.

delete.summary:
Revoke all active sessions for a user. Idempotent — safe to call when the user
has no sessions.

post.summary:
Check multiple permissions in a single request. When the parallel-batch pgrx extension
is loaded, single-hop checks are expanded into atomic (subject, relation, object_type,
object_id) tuples and evaluated in one BFS issuing D+1 SQL queries — independent of N.
Multi-hop permissions and deployments without the extension fall back to UNNEST grouping
(same path as POST /v1/authz/decisions).

get.summary:
Check whether the current session user (or an explicit user) has a permission
on a resource. Uses a bundled session-validation + authz CTE for one DB round-trip
when checking the current session user.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Explicit subject to check as. Defaults to the current session user.

parameters.name: permission
parameters.description: Permission name as defined in the authz schema (e.g. `"read"`, `"edit"`).

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: Resource type as defined in the authz schema.

parameters.name: resource_id
parameters.description: ID of the resource instance to check against.

get.summary:
List all objects of the given type that the current user (or an explicit user)
can access via the resolved roles for a permission. Includes both direct role
assignments and access granted via parent hierarchy.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Explicit subject to list objects for. Defaults to the current session user.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `next_page`.

post.summary: Write a single relation tuple. Idempotent — duplicate writes are silently ignored.

patch.summary: Batch write and/or delete relation tuples in a single transaction.

get.summary: Get the current authz schema. Returns null if authz is not enabled.

put.summary:
Replace the authz schema. Validates and compiles before persisting.
Setting schema to a valid document enables authz; this is the only way to enable it.

get.summary:
List all subjects with a given permission on a resource. Resolves through the
schema's role hierarchy to return every subject who can exercise the permission.
Only direct role assignments are expanded; access via parent hierarchy is not
included (use GET /v1/authz/objects from the subject's perspective for that).

parameters.name: permission
parameters.description: The permission to expand (e.g. `"edit"`). Resolves through the role hierarchy.

get.summary:
Explain why a permission check returned its result. Runs expand on all direct
role relations and reports which subjects appear, letting you trace a grant or
denial. Note: access granted purely via parent hierarchy is not reflected here.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Subject ID to trace access for.

get.summary: List all email addresses associated with the authenticated user.

post.summary:
Initiate a primary email address change. Returns a one-time token that the caller must
exchange via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=email_change`. The address is not
changed until the token is consumed. Returns 409 if the address is already registered.

Confirm email ownership using the token issued by `POST /v1/emails/{id}/verifications`.
Unauthenticated — the token itself identifies the user and email. Single-use; expires in 24 hours.

put.summary:
Promote a verified email address to primary. The address must belong to the authenticated
user and must already be verified. Returns 404 if not found or not yet verified.

404.description: Email not found or not verified

delete.summary:
Remove a non-primary email address. Returns 409 if the caller tries to delete their
primary address — use `PUT /v1/emails/{id}` to promote another address first.

post.summary:
Issue a verification token for an unverified email address. The caller is expected to
deliver this token to the user (e.g. via email) and have them confirm it via
`POST /v1/emails/verifications`. Returns 404 if the address doesn't exist or is already verified.

404.description: Email not found or already verified

get.summary:
List all authentication methods (identities) attached to the authenticated user.
Each identity represents one way the user can log in: a password, or a linked OAuth provider.

post.summary:
Add a password identity to an account that has none. Useful when a user signed up via
OAuth and wants to enable password login. Returns 409 if a password identity already exists.

delete.summary:
Unlink an authentication method. Returns 409 if this is the last identity — at least
one must remain so the user can still log in.

patch.summary:
Change the password for a password identity. Requires the current password. On success,
all sessions except the current one are revoked.

get.summary:
Look up an invitation by ID and token. Unauthenticated — intended for pre-acceptance
display (show the org name and role before asking the user to log in). Returns 404 if
the invitation doesn't exist, is expired, or the token is wrong.

post.summary:
Accept an invitation. The authenticated user is added to the org with the invitation's
role. The invitation token is consumed and cannot be reused. Returns 409 if already a member.

Decline an invitation. The token is consumed and the invitation is removed.
Unauthenticated — the token in the query string is sufficient.

get.summary:
The active JWT signing public keys in JWK Set format. Cached for 1 hour (`Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600`).
Use this endpoint to verify JWTs issued by `POST /v1/tokens`.

List all API keys for the authenticated user. Bearer token values are never returned
— only metadata (ID, name, created/used/expires timestamps).

post.summary:
Create an API key for the authenticated user. The full bearer token is returned in
`key` and is shown only once — store it immediately. Subsequent reads return only metadata.

get.summary: Get metadata for a single API key.

delete.summary: Revoke an API key. The key is immediately invalid for authentication.

post.summary:
Issue a passwordless magic-link token for the given email address. The caller is
responsible for delivering the token to the user (e.g. via email). The token is
exchanged for a session via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=magic_link`.
Expires in 15 minutes. Returns 404 if no account exists for the address.

Apple Sign-In callback. Apple uses a POST form submission instead of a GET redirect,
so this is a separate endpoint. Behavior is identical to `GET /v1/oauth/{provider}/callback`.

200.description: Link — identity added to existing account

get.summary:
Start an OAuth authorization flow. Returns the provider's authorization URL.
If the caller includes a valid session Bearer token, the resulting identity will be
linked to that account instead of creating a new session.

parameters.name: redirect_url
parameters.description: URL to redirect to after authentication

get.summary:
OAuth authorization callback. Exchanges the provider code for a profile, then either
creates a new session (login) or links the identity to the authenticated user (link flow).
When TOTP is enrolled, returns a step-up challenge instead of a session token.
Returns 409 if the OAuth identity is already claimed by a different user.

200.description: Link — identity added to existing account

409.description: OAuth identity already claimed by another user

get.summary: List all orgs the authenticated user is a member of, cursor-paginated.

post.summary:
Create a new organization. The authenticated user becomes the owner.
Returns 409 if the slug is already taken.

get.summary: Get an org by ID. Returns 403 if the authenticated user is not a member.

delete.summary:
Soft-delete an org. Requires owner role. Returns 409 if this is the user's personal org
(personal orgs are deleted via `DELETE /v1/users/me`).

patch.summary: Update an org. Requires owner role. Only fields present in the body are changed.

get.summary:
List pending invitations for an org, cursor-paginated. Requires owner.
Tokens are not included in list responses — only in creation and resend responses.

post.summary:
Create an invitation. Requires owner. The response includes a one-time `token` — deliver
it to the invitee so they can call `POST /v1/invitations/{id}/acceptances`. Returns 409
if a pending invitation already exists for the same email.

409.description: Pending invite for this email already exists

delete.summary: Revoke an invitation. The token is immediately invalidated. Requires owner.

post.summary:
Re-issue an invitation token. Invalidates the previous token and returns a fresh one.
Use this when the original token expires or is lost. Requires owner.

get.summary: List members of an org, cursor-paginated. Requires membership.

delete.summary:
Remove a member from an org. A user can remove themselves with only membership; removing
another member requires owner. Returns 409 if removing would leave the org with no owners.

patch.summary:
Update a member's role. Requires owner. Returns 409 if the change would leave the org
with no owners.

post.summary:
Begin WebAuthn passkey authentication (discoverable credential flow). Returns `options`
for `navigator.credentials.get()` and a `state_token`. Complete authentication by
posting `state_token` + credential to `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=passkey`.
Unauthenticated — no bearer token required.

Begin WebAuthn passkey registration. Returns a `options` object to pass directly to
`navigator.credentials.create()` and a `state_token` to include in the subsequent
`POST /v1/passkeys` finish call. Two-step: begin here, finish with `POST /v1/passkeys`.

get.summary: List all registered passkey credentials for the authenticated user.

post.summary:
Complete passkey registration. Submit the `state_token` from `POST /v1/passkey-registrations`
and the `PublicKeyCredential` response from `navigator.credentials.create()`.
The registered credential can then be used to authenticate via `POST /v1/passkey-authentications`.

delete.summary: Delete a registered passkey credential. The credential can no longer be used to authenticate.

patch.summary: Update the nickname of a registered passkey credential.

post.summary:
Issue a password-reset token for the given email address. The caller is responsible
for delivering the token to the user. The token is exchanged — along with a new password
— via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=password_reset`, which also invalidates all
existing sessions. Expires in 1 hour. Returns 404 if no account exists or the account
has no password identity.

404.description: No account with that email or no password identity

get.summary: List all active sessions for the authenticated user. The current session is included.

post.summary:
Authenticate and create a session. The `grant_type` field in the request body selects
the credential flow. On success, returns 201 with a session token. When the account has
TOTP enrolled and the grant type supports MFA, returns 200 with a `step_up_token` —
the caller must then re-POST with `grant_type=totp_step_up` to complete authentication.

delete.summary:
Revoke all sessions for the authenticated user. Use `except_current=true` to keep
the current session active (e.g. "sign out everywhere else").

parameters.name: except_current
parameters.description:
When true, the current session is preserved; all other sessions are revoked.
Defaults to false — all sessions including the current one are revoked.

get.summary: Return details about the session that authenticated the current request.

delete.summary:
Revoke the session that authenticated the current request. The bearer token becomes
immediately invalid. Idempotent — safe to call if the session is already gone.

Revoke a specific session by ID. The caller can only revoke their own sessions;
attempting to revoke another user's session returns 404 (not 403) to prevent enumeration.

post.summary: POST /v1/tokens — issue a short-lived JWT access token.
post.description:
Accepts any valid bearer credential in `Authorization`:
- **Session token** (`session_…`) — issues JWT + a fresh refresh token.
- **Refresh token** (`rt_…`) — rotates the refresh token and issues a new JWT.
- **API key** (`key_…`) — issues JWT only (no refresh token).

Requires `jwt_enabled = true` in app_config; returns 400 otherwise.
An optional JSON body may carry `claims` — a flat object of key/value pairs
merged into the JWT payload after all reserved claims are set.

post.summary:
Begin TOTP enrollment. Returns the secret and a QR code data URL. Enrollment is not
active until confirmed via `POST /v1/totp/confirmations`. Calling this again before
confirming replaces the pending enrollment.

delete.summary:
Disable TOTP for the authenticated user. Future logins will no longer require a step-up
challenge. All recovery codes are also invalidated.

post.summary:
Confirm TOTP enrollment by verifying a live code from the authenticator app. After this
call, TOTP is active and future logins will require a step-up challenge.

Regenerate TOTP recovery codes. Requires a valid TOTP code to prove the authenticator
app is still accessible. All existing recovery codes are invalidated and replaced.

Register a new user with email and password. Creates the user, a personal org, and
a session in a single transaction. Returns 409 if the email is already registered.

get.summary: Return the authenticated user's profile, primary email, and personal org.

delete.summary:
Soft-delete the authenticated user and their personal org. All active sessions are
invalidated immediately. This action is permanent — deleted accounts cannot be recovered.

patch.summary: Update the authenticated user's profile. Only fields present in the request body are changed.

AddPasswordRequest.description:
Request to add a password identity to an account that currently has none
(e.g. a user who signed up via OAuth and wants to add password login).

AddRequest.description: Request to initiate a primary email address change.

email.description: The new email address to switch to. Must not already be registered to any user.

AdminUserResponse.description: Full user record returned to admin callers.

deleted_at.description: Set when the user has been soft-deleted; null for active accounts.

AuthResponse.description:
Returned on successful login or signup. Contains the new session alongside the user,
primary email, and personal org context.

url.description: Full OAuth authorization URL — navigate the browser here to start the flow.

BatchRequest.description: Batch of relation tuples to write and/or delete in a single transaction.

deletes.description: Relation tuples to delete. Missing deletes are silently ignored.

writes.description: Relation tuples to create. Duplicate writes are silently ignored.

BatchResponse.description: Result counts from a batch relation operation.

options.description: WebAuthn options object — pass directly to the browser's WebAuthn API.

state_token.description: Opaque state token; include in the corresponding finish request.

CallbackResponse.description: Session token returned after a successful OAuth login callback.

token.description: Opaque session bearer token. Use as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

CheckResponse.description: Result of a single permission check.

allowed.description: True if the subject has the requested permission on the resource.

permission.description: Permission name as defined in the authz schema.

resource_id.description: ID of the resource instance to check against.

resource_type.description: Resource type as defined in the authz schema.

user.description: Explicit subject to check as. Defaults to the current session user.

results.description: Results in the same order as the input checks.

jwt_enabled.description: Whether `POST /v1/tokens` is enabled for JWT issuance.

session_idle_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds of inactivity before a session expires. Null means no idle timeout.

ConfirmRequest.description: Request body for TOTP confirmation and recovery-code regeneration.

code.description: Current 6-digit TOTP code from the authenticator app.

CreateInvitationRequest.description: Request to create an invitation to the org.

email.description:
Email address to address the invitation to. Omit for an open link invitation
that any user can accept.

role.description: Role the invitee will receive on acceptance.

CreateOrgRequest.description: Request to create a new organization.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier. Defaults to a slugified version of `name` if omitted.

expires_at.description: When the key expires. Defaults to 100 years from now (effectively never).

key.description: The full bearer token. Store it now — it cannot be retrieved later.

CreateResponse.description: Returned only on creation — the `key` field is never shown again.

CurrentSessionResponse.description: Details of the session that authenticated the current request.

ip_address.description: IP address recorded at session creation, if available.

last_used_at.description: Time the bearer token was last presented, updated on each authenticated request.

token_id.description: ID of the underlying bearer token used to authenticate this request.

user_agent.description: User-Agent recorded at session creation, if available.

EmailBody.description: Primary email address for the user.

verified_at.description: Null if the address has not been verified.

EmailRecord.description: An email address belonging to the authenticated user.

is_primary.description: True for the address that appears in `AuthResponse.email` and receives system emails.

verified_at.description: Null if the address has not been verified yet.

EnrollmentResponse.description:
TOTP enrollment data returned when starting enrollment. Pass `provisioning_uri` or
`qr_data_url` to an authenticator app, then confirm enrollment with a live TOTP code.

provisioning_uri.description: `otpauth://` URI for QR code generation or direct authenticator import.

qr_data_url.description: Data URL (`data:image/png;base64,…`) of the provisioning QR code.

recovery_codes.description: Single-use recovery codes. Store these securely — they are shown only once.

secret_b32.description: Raw TOTP secret in base32 — for manual entry into an authenticator app.

ErrorBody.description: Wire-format error body returned on all non-2xx responses.

ErrorResponse.description: Top-level error envelope for all API error responses.

status.description: `"ok"` when healthy, `"degraded"` when the database is unreachable.

parent_relation.description: The relation name on this resource that points to parent objects.

parent_resource.description: The resource type of the parent.

display.description: Human-readable label: email address for password identities, provider slug for OAuth.

user_id.description: The ID of the user to impersonate.

InvitationResponse.description: An org invitation. On creation, `token` is populated — it is never returned again.

email.description: Email address the invitation is addressed to, if any. Null for open link invitations.

role.description: Role the invitee will receive on acceptance.

token.description: Plaintext token — only present on creation, never returned again. Deliver this to the invitee.

InvitationViewResponse.description: Public view of an invitation, shown to the invitee before they accept or decline.

role.description: The role the invitee will receive on acceptance.

InvitationsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of pending org invitations.

next_page.description: Opaque cursor — pass as `after` to retrieve the next page.

claims.description:
Arbitrary JSON object merged into the JWT payload.
Reserved keys (`sub`, `iss`, `aud`, `iat`, `nbf`, `exp`, `jti`,
`impersonated`) are always overwritten by the service and cannot be
supplied here.

Jwk.description: A single JSON Web Key (Ed25519 public key in JWK format).

kid.description: Key ID matching the `kid` claim in issued JWTs.

JwkSet.description: JSON Web Key Set — the public keys used to verify JWTs issued by this service.

LinkCallbackResponse.description:
Returned when an OAuth callback links a provider identity to an existing account
rather than creating a new session.

oneOf.description:
Email + password login. If TOTP is enrolled, returns 200 with a `step_up_token`
instead of a session — caller must complete MFA via `totp_step_up`.
Exchanges a token issued by `POST /v1/magic-links`. Single-use; expires in 15 min.

token.description: The plaintext token from the magic-link response.

oneOf.description:
Exchanges a password-reset token, sets the new password, and creates a session.
All existing sessions are invalidated.

token.description: The plaintext token from the password-reset response.

oneOf.description:
Exchanges an email-change token, promotes the new address to primary, and creates
a session. All existing sessions are invalidated.

token.description: The plaintext token from the email-change (`POST /v1/emails`) response.

oneOf.description: Completes a TOTP MFA step-up. Present after a 200 response with `step_up_required`.

code.description: Six-digit TOTP code from the authenticator app.

step_up_token.description: The `step_up_token` from the preceding 200 step-up response.

oneOf.description: Completes MFA using a single-use TOTP recovery code. Consumes the code permanently.

code.description: One of the recovery codes issued at TOTP enrollment or last regeneration.

step_up_token.description: The `step_up_token` from the preceding 200 step-up response.

oneOf.description: Completes a WebAuthn passkey authentication flow.

LoginRequest.description: Credential payload for session creation. The `grant_type` field discriminates the variant.

MagicLinkRequest.description: Request to issue a magic-link login token for a user.

MagicLinkResponse.description:
Magic-link token response. Pass `token` to `POST /v1/sessions` with
`grant_type=magic_link` to authenticate.

token.description: One-time token to exchange via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=magic_link`.

org.description: The user's personal org. Multi-org memberships are listed via `GET /v1/orgs`.

role.description: Role within this org, e.g. `"owner"` or `"member"`.

next_page.description: Opaque cursor — pass as `after` to retrieve the next page.

ObjectsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of resource IDs the subject can access.

next_page.description: Opaque cursor — pass as `after` for the next page.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier, unique across all orgs.

OrgResponse.description: An organization the authenticated user is a member of.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier, unique across all orgs.

next_page.description: Opaque cursor — pass as `after` to retrieve the next page.

OttTokenResponse.description: One-time token response for flows that require a follow-up session grant.

token.description: One-time token to use in the corresponding session grant.

PasswordResetRequest.description: Request to issue a password-reset token for a user.

PasswordResetResponse.description:
Password-reset token response. Pass `token` to `POST /v1/sessions` with
`grant_type=password_reset` along with the new password to complete the reset.

token.description: One-time token to exchange via `POST /v1/sessions` with `grant_type=password_reset`.

recovery_codes.description: New single-use recovery codes. All previous codes are immediately invalidated.

RelationObject.description: The resource side of a relation tuple.

id.description: Unique identifier for this resource instance.

type.description: Resource type as defined in the authz schema.

relation.description: The relation name as defined in the authz schema (e.g. `"owner"`, `"member"`).

RelationSubject.description: The subject (actor) side of a relation tuple.

id.description: Subject ID — typically a user ID or another resource ID for subject sets.

relation.description: For subject sets: the relation on the subject resource that grants membership.

type.description: For subject sets: the type of the subject resource. Omit for direct user subjects.

SessionBody.description: Session credential returned on login or signup.

token.description: Opaque bearer token — store securely, transmit as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

current.description: Whether this is the caller's current session.

SessionsResponse.description: Paginated list of active sessions for the authenticated user.

SignupRequest.description: Registration request. Creates a user, a personal org, and a session in one call.

display_name.description: Display name for the user's personal org. Defaults to the local part of the email.

StepUpResponse.description: Returned when the user has TOTP enrolled — caller must complete the step-up flow.

step_up_token.description: Short-lived signed token to present when completing the step-up.

Subject.description: A subject (actor) with the relation through which they hold access.

relation.description: The direct relation through which this subject was found.

SubjectsResponse.description: Subjects who hold the queried relation or permission on a resource.

refresh_token.description:
Rotate-on-use refresh token. Present when authenticated via a session or
a prior refresh token; absent for API-key auth.

TraceResponse.description: Trace result explaining why a permission check returned its outcome.

allowed.description: Whether the specified user has the permission on the resource.

subjects.description: All subjects found during expansion — check if `user` appears here to understand the grant.

UpdateConfigRequest.description:
Partial config update. Only fields explicitly set are updated.
Send `"session_idle_timeout_seconds": null` to clear the idle timeout.
Partial config update. Only fields present in the body are changed.
Send `"session_idle_timeout_seconds": null` to clear the idle timeout.

jwt_enabled.description: When true, `POST /v1/tokens` issues JWT access tokens.

session_idle_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds of inactivity before a session is considered expired. Send `null` to disable.

UpdateCredentialRequest.description: Request to rename a registered passkey credential.

nickname.description: Human-readable label shown in credential lists.

UpdateIdentityRequest.description:
Request to change the password for an existing password identity.
Requires the current password as proof of possession.

current_password.description: The existing password — must be correct or the request is rejected with 401.

UpdateMeRequest.description:
Partial update for the user's personal org. All fields are optional; omitted fields
are left unchanged.

metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON merged into the org's metadata field (full replacement, not merge).

name.description: Display name for the user and their personal org.

slug.description: URL-safe org identifier. Must be unique. Returns 409 if already taken.

UpdateMemberRequest.description: Request to change a member's role.

role.description: New role for the member, e.g. `"owner"` or `"member"`.

UpdateOrgRequest.description: Partial org update. Omitted fields are left unchanged.

metadata.description: Full replacement of the org's metadata field.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier. Returns 409 if already taken.

metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON metadata stored on the personal org.

name.description: Display name (mirrors the personal org name).

primary_org_id.description: ID of the user's personal org. Always present; created at signup.

tags.name: identities
tags.description: Auth method management — list, add password, update, unlink
